chapter_title.en: The Etiquette of Women in Prayer

parts.text: سَأَلَ يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ:

text: ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قَالَ قُلْتُ فَالْمَرْأَةُ قَالَ «لاَ وَ لاَ يَصْلُحُ لِلْحُرَّةِ إِذَا حَاضَتْ إِلاَّ اَلْخِمَارُ إِلاَّ أَنْ لاَ تَجِدَهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1082 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who prays in one garment.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
He then asked: "What about a woman?"
Imam (as) said: "No, and it is not proper for a free woman (Hurr) to pray when she menstruates except with a khimar (headscarf), unless She does not find it."
